member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.AnthropicClient.#ctor(Anthropic.SDK.APIAuthentication,System.Net.Http.HttpClient,Anthropic.SDK.IRequestInterceptor)
member.summary: Creates a new entry point to the Anthropic API, handling auth and allowing access to the various API endpoints
member.param: The API authentication information to use for API calls, or to attempt to use the , potentially loading from environment vars.
A .

Optional request interceptor for advanced scenarios like custom retry logic, logging, or auditing. Most users don't need this - the SDK works perfectly without it. Only provide this if you need to intercept and customize HTTP requests before they're sent.

remarks.para: implements to manage the lifecycle of the resources it uses, including . When you initialize , it will create an internal instance if one is not provided. This internal HttpClient is disposed of when AnthropicClient is disposed of. If you provide an external HttpClient instance to AnthropicClient, you are responsible for managing its disposal.

Request Interceptor: This is an advanced feature for scenarios requiring custom request handling. Common use cases include:
Custom retry logic beyond what HttpClient provides
Special logging/tracing requirements
Company-specific audit requirements
Request/response modification or inspection
If you're already handling resilience at the HttpClient level (via Polly, HttpClientFactory policies, etc.), you typically don't need a request interceptor.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.AnthropicClient.Messages
member.summary: Text generation is the core function of the API. You give the API a prompt, and it generates a completion. The way you “program” the API to do a task is by simply describing the task in plain english or providing a few written examples. This simple approach works for a wide range of use cases, including summarization, translation, grammar correction, question answering, chatbots, composing emails, and much more (see the prompt library for inspiration).

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.AnthropicClient.Batches
member.summary: Batches are a way to send multiple requests to the API at once. This is useful for when you have a large number of requests to make, or when you want to make multiple requests in parallel.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.AnthropicClient.Models
member.summary: Models are a way to manage the models that the API uses to generate completions. You can list models, as well as get information about a specific model.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.AnthropicClient.Files
member.summary: Files API allows you to download, list, and manage Claude-generated files.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.AnthropicClient.Skills
member.summary: Skills API allows you to create, list, retrieve, and delete custom skills that extend Claude's capabilities.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Common.Function.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonNode)
member.summary: Creates a new function description to insert into a chat conversation.
member.param:
Required. The name of the function to generate arguments for based on the context in a message.
May contain a-z, A-Z, 0-9, underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64 characters. Recommended to not begin with a number or a dash.
An optional description of the function, used by the API to determine if it is useful to include in the response.
An optional JSON object describing the parameters of the function that the model can generate.

member.summary: Get or create a tool from a static method.

member.summary: Clones an HttpRequestMessage using pre-captured content.

member.summary: Sets thinking parameters for extended thinking support in compatible models like Claude 3.7 Sonnet

The budget tokens for thinking (typically up to max_tokens unless using interleaved thinking)

The thinking parameters to set

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.ChatOptionsExtensions.WithInterleavedThinking(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets interleaved thinking parameters for enhanced thinking support. This enables the interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14 beta header which allows thinking tokens to exceed max_tokens. Note: On 3rd-party platforms (Bedrock, Vertex AI), this only works with Claude Opus 4.1, Opus 4, or Sonnet 4 models.
member.param: The ChatOptions instance
The budget tokens for thinking (can exceed max_tokens when using interleaved thinking)
member.returns: The ChatOptions instance for fluent chaining

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.ChatOptionsExtensions.WithInterleavedThinking(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ThinkingParameters)
member.summary: Sets interleaved thinking parameters for enhanced thinking support. This enables the interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14 beta header which allows thinking tokens to exceed max_tokens. Note: On 3rd-party platforms (Bedrock, Vertex AI), this only works with Claude Opus 4.1, Opus 4, or Sonnet 4 models.
member.param: The ChatOptions instance
The thinking parameters to set
member.returns: The ChatOptions instance for fluent chaining

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.ChatOptionsExtensions.WithAdaptiveThinking(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions)
member.summary: Sets adaptive thinking mode, which lets Claude dynamically determine when and how much to use extended thinking. Recommended for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6. Interleaved thinking is automatically enabled.
member.param: The ChatOptions instance
member.returns: The ChatOptions instance for fluent chaining

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.ChatOptionsExtensions.WithAdaptiveThinking(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ThinkingEffort)
member.summary: Sets adaptive thinking mode with a specific effort level. The effort level is mapped to output_config.effort to guide how much thinking Claude does. Recommended for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6.
member.param: The ChatOptions instance
The effort level (low, medium, high, or max) to guide thinking allocation
member.returns: The ChatOptions instance for fluent chaining

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.ChatOptionsExtensions.GetThinkingParameters(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions)
member.summary: Gets the thinking parameters from ChatOptions
member.param: The ChatOptions instance
member.returns: The thinking parameters, or null if not set

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.ChatOptionsExtensions.WithStrictTools(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Enables strict mode for tool use, which guarantees that tool inputs conform exactly to the schema. Requires the structured-outputs-2025-11-13 beta header (automatically added). Note: Strict tools are automatically enabled when using ResponseFormat with a JSON schema.
member.param: The ChatOptions instance
Whether to enable strict mode (default: true)
member.returns: The ChatOptions instance for fluent chaining

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.ChatOptionsExtensions.GetStrictToolsEnabled(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions)
member.summary: Gets whether strict tools mode is enabled from ChatOptions

member.summary: Cost of web search requests ($0.01 per search).

member.name: T:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.CostCalculationExtensions

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.CostCalculationExtensions.CalculateCost(Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.Usage,System.String,Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing)
member.summary: Calculate the estimated cost of an API request from its data. When the service tier is , a 50% discount is applied to all token costs automatically.
member.param: The usage data from the API response.
The model ID string used for the request.
Optional pricing to use instead of the built-in/registered pricing.
member.returns: A with per-category costs.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when no pricing can be found for and is not provided.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Extensions.CostCalculationExtensions.CalculateCost(Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.MessageResponse,Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing)
member.summary: Calculate the estimated cost of an API request directly from the . Uses the model from the response and its usage data.
member.param: The message response from the API.
Optional pricing to use instead of the built-in/registered pricing.
member.returns: A with per-category costs.
member.exception: Thrown when or its Usage is null.

member.summary: Downloads all file outputs from bash code execution results to the specified directory path. This is a convenience method that automatically iterates through response content, identifies file outputs, and downloads them using the Files API.
member.param: The message response containing potential file outputs.
The AnthropicClient instance to use for downloading files.
The directory path where files should be downloaded. If the directory doesn't exist, it will be created.
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: A list of full file paths for all downloaded files.
member.exception: Thrown when response, client, or outputPath is null or empty.
Thrown when the output directory cannot be created.

member.name: T:Anthropic.SDK.IRequestInterceptor
member.summary: Interface for intercepting HTTP requests made by the Anthropic SDK. Allows users to implement custom retry logic, logging, metrics, or other cross-cutting concerns.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.IRequestInterceptor.InvokeAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,System.Func{System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Net.Http.HttpResponseMessage}},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Intercepts an HTTP request and optionally wraps the call with custom logic.
member.param: The HTTP request about to be sent
A delegate to invoke the next handler in the chain (or the actual HTTP call). Takes the request and cancellation token as parameters.
Cancellation token for the operation
member.returns: The HTTP response from the request
member.remarks: This method allows you to add retry logic, circuit breakers, logging, metrics, or other cross-cutting concerns. The interceptor is invoked for every HTTP request made by the SDK. You must call the next delegate to proceed with the actual HTTP request. The interceptor can modify the request before passing it to the next handler.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.Message.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.List{Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.MessageResponse})
member.summary: Reconstructs a single Message from a list of streaming MessageResponse events. Uses a single ordered pass to preserve the natural stream order of content blocks, which is required for multi-turn conversations where the API validates that every server_tool_use has its corresponding result block in the correct position.

member.summary: Cost per million input tokens.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.OutputTokenCostPerMillion
member.summary: Cost per million output tokens.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.CacheReadCostPerMillion
member.summary: Cost per million cache read tokens (typically 0.1x input cost).

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.Cache5mWriteCostPerMillion
member.summary: Cost per million 5-minute cache write tokens (typically 1.25x input cost).

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.Cache1hWriteCostPerMillion
member.summary: Cost per million 1-hour cache write tokens (typically 2x input cost).

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.WebSearchCostPer1000
member.summary: Cost per 1,000 web search requests. Default is $10 per 1,000 searches.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.Register(System.String,Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing)
member.summary: Register or override pricing for a model ID prefix. Custom registrations take priority over built-in pricing.
member.param: The model ID or prefix to match (e.g. "claude-sonnet-4-6").
The pricing to use for matching models.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.Unregister(System.String)
member.summary: Remove a previously registered custom pricing entry.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.ClearCustomPricing
member.summary: Clear all custom pricing registrations, reverting to built-in pricing only.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Messaging.ModelPricing.ForModel(System.String)
member.summary: Look up pricing for a given model ID. Custom registrations are checked first (longest prefix match), then built-in pricing.
member.returns: The matching , or null if no match is found.

member.summary: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the skill was created.

member.summary: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the skill was last updated.

member.name: T:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillsEndpoint
member.summary: Skills endpoint for managing custom skills. The Skills API allows you to create, list, retrieve, and delete custom skills that extend Claude's capabilities.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillsEndpoint.#ctor(Anthropic.SDK.AnthropicClient)
member.summary: Constructor of the api endpoint. Rather than instantiating this yourself, access it through an instance of as .

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillsEndpoint.CreateSkillAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a new custom skill by uploading skill files. All files must be in the same top-level directory and must include a SKILL.md file at the root of that directory.
member.param: Display title for the skill. This is a human-readable label that is not included in the prompt sent to the model.
Path to the directory containing skill files. Must include a SKILL.md file.
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: The created skill response.
member.exception: Thrown when parameters are invalid or SKILL.md is not found.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillsEndpoint.CreateSkillFromZipAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a new custom skill by uploading a zip file. The zip file must contain a SKILL.md file at its root.
member.param: Display title for the skill. This is a human-readable label that is not included in the prompt sent to the model.
Path to the zip file containing skill files. Must include a SKILL.md file.
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: The created skill response.
member.exception: Thrown when parameters are invalid.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillsEndpoint.CreateSkillFromStreamsAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.ValueTuple{System.String,System.IO.Stream,System.String}},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a new custom skill by uploading file streams. Files must include a SKILL.md file.
member.param: Display title for the skill. This is a human-readable label that is not included in the prompt sent to the model.
List of tuples containing (filename, stream, mimeType) for each file to upload.
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: The created skill response.
member.exception: Thrown when parameters are invalid.
Thrown when files is null or empty.

Path to the directory containing skill files. Must include a SKILL.md file.
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: The created skill version response.
member.exception: Thrown when parameters are invalid or SKILL.md is not found.

Path to the zip file containing skill files. Must include a SKILL.md file.
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: The created skill version response.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillsEndpoint.ListSkillVersionsAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lists all versions of a specific skill. Supports pagination using page tokens.
member.param: The ID of the skill to list versions for.
Pagination token for fetching a specific page of results. Optionally set to the next_page token from the previous response.
Number of items to return per page. Defaults to 20. Ranges from 1 to 1000.
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: A paginated list of skill version objects.

Thrown when limit is outside the valid range.

Version identifier for the skill. Each version is identified by a Unix epoch timestamp (e.g., "1759178010641129").
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: The skill version response.
member.exception: Thrown when skillId or version is null or empty.

member.name: M:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillsEndpoint.ListSkillsAsync(System.String,System.Int32,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lists all skills in the organization. Supports pagination using page tokens.
member.param: Pagination token for fetching a specific page of results. Pass the value from a previous response's next_page field to get the next page of results.
Number of results to return per page. Maximum value is 100. Defaults to 20.
Filter skills by source. If provided, only skills from the specified source will be returned: "custom" for user-created skills, "anthropic" for Anthropic-created skills.
Optional cancellation token.
member.returns: A paginated list of skill objects.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillVersionResponse.Name
member.summary: Human-readable name of the skill version. This is extracted from the SKILL.md file in the skill upload.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillVersionResponse.Description
member.summary: Description of the skill version. This is extracted from the SKILL.md file in the skill upload.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillVersionResponse.Directory
member.summary: Directory name of the skill version. This is the top-level directory name that was extracted from the uploaded files.

member.name: P:Anthropic.SDK.Skills.SkillVersionResponse.CreatedAt
member.summary: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the skill version was created.
